specs.ranges: 0-100 ppm (Low range of the 106 industrial/ambient series)

-M = Medium range (0-1,000 ppm) variant of the 106 ozone monitor series, for higher-concentration ozone (ozone generation/disinfection). Range confirmed on manufacturer/distributor listings; draft's 2 s response nulled as unverified.

-MH = Medium-High range (0-10,000 ppm) variant of the 106 series for high-concentration ozone (ozone generators, industrial). Distinct from the 106-H (High, 0-20 wt%). Not used for ambient air. Range confirmed on manufacturer/distributor listings; draft's 2 s response nulled as unverified.

specs.ranges: 0-250,000 ppb (0-250 ppm) selectable; typical use 0-250 ppb
specs.lod: Precision greater of 1.5 ppb or 2% of reading
specs.notes: Single-cell UV absorption at 254 nm. Lightweight (~2 kg), low power; widely used for portable and network ambient ozone monitoring. Averaging times selectable 10 s to hours. EPA FEM designation EQOA-0410-190 confirmed.

specs.ranges: 0-250,000 ppb (0-250 ppm) selectable; typical 0-250 ppb / 0-2000 ppb
specs.lod: 2.0 ppb; precision greater of 1.0 ppb or 2% of reading (10-s avg)
specs.notes: Dual-beam design measures reference and sample cells simultaneously for improved precision (~1 ppb) and faster response (~4 s, 2-s measurement frequency) than single-beam Model 202. 254 nm UV absorption. Shares EPA FEM designation EQOA-0410-190 with the Model 202 (approved as a modification of the 202). CORRECTED: draft's EQOA-0815-227 is actually the POM's designation, not the 205's.

principle: UV photometry with gas-phase (NO titration) scrubber

specs.notes: Scrubberless design: instead of a solid-phase (e.g. MnO2) scrubber, ~5 ppm NO is pulsed into the sample every ~2 s to titrate ozone (NO + O3 -> NO2 + O2) as the reference, removing interference from UV-absorbing species. ~30 cm path. Best suited to heavily polluted air where VOC/mercury/particulate interferences are likely. Principle confirmed on manufacturer datasheet.

principle: UV photometry (254 nm, dual beam, heated graphite scrubber)

specs.notes: Variant of the Model 211 that replaces the gas-phase NO titration scrubber with a patented heated graphite scrubber (U.S. Patent 10,295,517) for interference-free measurement in polluted air (aromatic VOCs, Hg); precision greater of 0.5 ppb or 1% of reading (10 s avg); response 4 s in 2-s mode; flow ~2 L/min; covered under the same FEM designation as the Model 211 for 0-0.5 ppm at 20-30 C (EPA lists 'Model 211 and 211-G Ozone Monitors' under EQOA-0514-215); full measurement range not verified here

principle: Photolytic ozone generation (UV photolysis of O2 at 185 nm), NIST-traceable transfer source

specs.ranges: O3 output: 0 ppb and 30-1,000 ppb at 4.0 L/min output flow
specs.notes: Precision/accuracy: greater of 2.0 ppb or 2% of set concentration; output compensated for ambient temperature, pressure and humidity; programmable multi-step autocalibration scheduler (up to 10 steps); portable field calibrator for ambient ozone analyzers of any manufacturer; shipped with NIST-traceable calibration

principle: Direct optical absorption at 405 nm for NO2; NO by O3 conversion of NO to NO2 (by difference); NOx = NO + NO2

specs.notes: NO2 measured directly by absorbance at 405 nm (~2 m folded optical path); NO measured by adding ozone to convert NO to NO2 and taking the difference; NOx = NO + NO2. Avoids molybdenum/photolytic-converter efficiency issues of chemiluminescence NOx monitors. Principle confirmed on manufacturer datasheet.

principle: Photolytic NO source (UV photolysis of N2O by low-pressure Hg lamp at ~185 nm)

specs.ranges: NO output: 0 ppb and 20-1,000 ppb (stated range 10-1,000 ppb elsewhere)
specs.precision: higher of 3.0 ppb or 3% of concentration
specs.notes: Accuracy: higher of 3.0 ppb or 3% of concentration. Rise time is 30 s to reach 95% of selected concentration. Sample device draw must be <3.0 L/min with excess vented. Factory calibrated against NIST-traceable standard; can be used as a transfer standard. Produces NO in ambient air; output also contains ~3% N2O.

principle: Ozone-based measurement (NO + O3 -> NO2 + O2; resulting ozone depletion measured by UV absorption at 254 nm)

specs.notes: CORRECTED PRINCIPLE: Model 410 NO monitor measures NO by reacting it with added ozone and detecting the resulting ozone depletion by UV absorbance at 254 nm (same UV/ozone-monitor platform, an absolute method requiring infrequent calibration) - it is NOT the 405 nm NO2-detection platform as the draft stated. Replaces the Model 400 (~2x better precision). With the Model 401 NO2 Converter, measures NOx and NO2 (by difference). Model confirmed to exist via manufacturer brochure/manual; draft under-confidence corrected upward.

principle: Gas-phase titration (GPT) calibrator: photolytic O3 source (185 nm photolysis of O2) plus NO from UV photolysis of N2O; NO2 by stoichiometric reaction of O3 with excess NO

specs.notes: Combines the Model 306 ozone source and Model 408 NO source in one unit; precision/accuracy greater of 2.0 ppb or 2% of NO or O3 concentration; output flow ~2.0-2.7 L/min; N2O supplied from small disposable cartridges or cylinder (no large compressed-gas cylinder needed); ~7.5 kg; Modbus TCP/IP via Ethernet for remote operation; NIST-traceable; described in Birks et al., Atmos. Meas. Tech. 13, 1001-1018, 2020

specs.ranges: LOD ~4 ppb up to 0-10 ppm; typical 0-1000 ppb

principle: UV resonance absorption spectroscopy (UV-RAS) / differential UV photometry

principle: off-axis ICOS (cavity-enhanced laser absorption, direct NO2)

Packages verified against LGR datasheet: 908-0009 benchtop, 907-0009 rackmount standard, 911-0009 rackmount EP. R&D 100 Award 2012 (developed with Harvard University). Continues in ABB's current line as the OA-ICOS NUV rackmount GLA211 Series (GLA211 NO2-907).

Confirmed real Thermo product (catalog code 42IQTL). Trace-level iQ-series NOx analyzer; LOD value reflects Thermo's published ~25 ppt (0.025 ppb) zero noise at 120 s averaging rather than a formally stated LDL.

specs.ranges: 0-0.05 to 0-100 ppm full-scale (selectable); trace-level 43C-TLE configurable to sub-ppb (0-50 ppb and up under EPA equivalent-method conditions)
specs.lod: ~1 ppb (43C standard); ~0.05 ppb / 50 ppt (43C-TLE trace-level enhanced)
specs.notes: Legacy C-series pulsed UV fluorescence SO2 analyzer. The aromatic-hydrocarbon 'kicker'/cutter that removes aromatic-HC interference is standard across the pulsed-fluorescence 43 line (present even on the original Model 43); -TLE = trace-level enhanced (improved sensitivity/optics for sub-ppb), not the sole location of the kicker as the draft implied. Predecessor to 43i/43i-TLE. CORRECTED EPA designation: the EPA AMTIC list assigns the 43C (with 43A/43B/43C-TLE/43i/43i-TLE) to EQSA-0486-060; the draft's EQSA-0276-009 applies ONLY to the original plain 'Thermo Electron Model 43', not the 43C. Also removed the unattested '43C-TL' alias (the C-series SO2 trace variant is 43C-TLE per EPA).
Pulsed UV fluorescence SO2 analyzer; standard ambient configuration.
Trace-level enhanced SO2 analyzer for background/clean-air monitoring; widely used in regulatory trace SO2 networks. EPA designation EQSA-0486-060 explicitly covers the 43i-TLE (verified).
iQ-series pulsed UV fluorescence SO2 analyzer; successor to 43i. LOD value not independently verified.

specs.ranges: 0-1 to 0-10,000 ppm full-scale (selectable); trace-level 48C-TL configurable to low-ppm/sub-ppm
specs.lod: ~0.10 ppm / 100 ppb (48C standard); ~0.04 ppm / 40 ppb (48C-TL trace-level)
specs.notes: Legacy C-series non-dispersive infrared gas-filter-correlation CO analyzer (4.6 um). -TL = trace-level variant (EPA index also lists an enhanced 48C-TLE). Predecessor to the 48i. EPA CO reference method RFCA-0981-054 (Sept 1981) explicitly covers Models 48, 48C, 48C-TLE, 48i, 48i-TLE per the EPA AMTIC list.
Gas filter correlation IR CO analyzer; standard ambient configuration. Manufacturer LDL 0.04 ppm.

Enhanced trace-level gas filter correlation CO analyzer for lower ambient concentrations. EPA designation RFCA-0981-054 explicitly covers the 48iTLE (verified). Trace-level LOD (~20 ppb) is plausible vs 40 ppb standard but not independently confirmed.
iQ-series gas filter correlation CO analyzer; successor to 48i. LOD value not independently verified.

specs.ranges: 0-0.05 to 0-200 ppm full-scale (selectable); typical ambient range 0-1 ppm
specs.notes: Legacy C-series UV photometric (254 nm absorption) ozone analyzer. The 49C-PS is the ozone photometric primary/transfer-standard (calibrator) variant. Predecessor to the 49i. EPA O3 automated equivalent method EQOA-0880-047 (Aug 1980) explicitly covers Models 49, 49C, 49i per the EPA AMTIC list.
Dual-cell UV absorption (254 nm) ozone analyzer; standard ambient configuration.
Ozone primary/transfer standard used to calibrate ambient ozone monitors; internal UV photometer plus ozone generator. No EPA ambient-method designation (it is a calibration standard, not a monitor).
iQ-series dual-cell UV absorption ozone analyzer; successor to 49i. LOD value not independently verified.

Confirmed real. Direct methane and non-methane hydrocarbon (NMHC) analyzer using flame ionization detection with a back-flush GC column (eight-port rotary valve) to separate methane from NMHC; ~70 s measurement cycle. Draft's 'catalytic' separation description corrected to GC back-flush. Used in ambient and CEMS applications.

Percent-level CARBOCAP CO2 probe (0-20 %CO2) for high concentrations: incubators, chambers, biogas. Range corrected from draft's erroneous '0-10000/0-20000 ppm' — GMP251 is a %-level, not ppm-level, probe (GMP252 is the ppm-level version).

specs.ranges: CO2 0-10000 ppm (up to 30000 ppm with reduced accuracy)
specs.notes: Digital ppm-level CARBOCAP CO2 probe; used in environmental/soil chamber and ambient CO2 measurements. Range corrected: single 0-10000 ppm range (draft's '0-1000/0-10000 ppm' was inaccurate).
Diffusion or flow-through CO2 probe; widely used in soil/canopy CO2 profiles and ambient monitoring. Accuracy roughly +/-(3 ppm + 1% of reading).
